title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing · Contract Workshop
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds risk-layered Bookbot test suite from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of risk-layered Bookbot test suite while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.
Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Test Partitions
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 7545 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-lsmg5z
# Competency: bookbot-test-quality.
# Case 7335: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_test_quality_lsmg5z(unit_cases: int = 18, integration_cases: int = 7, cli_cases: int = 8, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, installed_smoke: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_lsmg5z = "lsmg5z-z5gmsl-7335"
    assert evidence_variant_lsmg5z
    evidence_axes_lsmg5z = (
        "corpuslsmg5z",
        "policyz5gmsl",
        "revision7335",
        "commandlsmg5z7335",
        "reportz5gmsl7335",
        "test7335lsmg5z",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_lsmg5z)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, integration_cases, cli_cases) < 1:
        return False, 0, "test-layers"
    if integration_cases < 2:
        return False, integration_cases, "filesystem-evidence"
    if cli_cases < 2:
        return False, cli_cases, "cli-evidence"
    if not changed_defect_detected or not installed_smoke:
        return False, unit_cases, "weak-suite"
    total = unit_cases + integration_cases + cli_cases
    assert total >= 7
    return True, total, "risk-layered-tests"
